= Reference
:navtitle: Reference Overview

Reference material is _information-oriented_: exhaustive, accurate, and dry. The reader is looking
something up, not learning. Keep narrative out; put it in tutorials or explanations instead.

== What lives here

* *REST API reference* — request/response schemas for every endpoint (auto-generated, see below).
* *Configuration reference* — every environment variable, its default, and its effect.
* *CLI reference* — commands, flags, and exit codes, if the project ships a CLI.

== Auto-generating the API reference from OpenAPI

Do not hand-write endpoint docs. Generate AsciiDoc from the service's OpenAPI schema so the
reference can never drift from the code. The pipeline (mirrored from the reference project) is:

. The service exports its OpenAPI schema (`make openapi`) — a FastAPI app can dump it from
  `app.openapi()`.
. The `openapi-generator-cli` `asciidoc` generator renders the schema into pages under
  `modules/ROOT/pages/api-docs/`.
. A `make api-docs` target wraps both steps; `make update-api-docs` (in the docs repo) clones the
  service, runs `api-docs`, and copies the result in.

[source,bash]
----
make openapi      # export the OpenAPI schema(s) to resources/
make api-docs     # render AsciiDoc into modules/ROOT/pages/api-docs/
----

[NOTE]
====
Generated API pages are build artefacts. Add the generated `api-docs/` subtree to the docs
repo's `.gitignore` (or commit it deliberately and regenerate in CI) — but never edit it by hand.
====

== Configuration reference (skeleton)

[cols="2,1,4",options="header"]
|===
| Variable | Default | Description

| `LOG_LEVEL` | `INFO` | Application log level.
| `REQUEST_TIMEOUT_SECONDS` | `30` | Per-request upstream timeout.
|===
